2605.04882.title: FairEnc: A Fair Vision-Language Model with Fair Vision and Text Encoders for Glaucoma Detection
2605.04882.paper_summary: Automated glaucoma detection is critical for preventing irreversible vision loss and reducing the burden on healthcare systems. However, ensuring fairness across diverse patient populations remains a significant challenge. In this paper, we propose FairEnc, a fair pretraining method for vision-language models (VLMs) that enables simultaneous debiasing across multiple sensitive attributes. FairEnc jointly mitigates biases in both textual and visual modalities with respect to multiple sensitive attributes, including race, gender, ethnicity, and language. Specifically, for the textual encoder, we leverage a large language model to generate synthetic clinical descriptions with varied sensitive attributes while preserving disease semantics, and employ a contrastive alignment objective to encourage demographic-invariant representations. For the visual encoder, we propose a dual-level fairness strategy that combines mutual information regularization to reduce statistical dependence between learned features and demographic groups, with multi-discriminator adversarial debiasing. Comprehensive experiments on the publicly available Harvard-FairVLMed dataset demonstrate that FairEnc effectively reduces demographic disparity as measured by DPD and DEOdds while achieving strong diagnostic performance under both zero-shot and linear probing evaluations. Additional experiments on the private FairFundus dataset show that FairEnc consistently preserves fairness advantages under cross-domain and cross-modality settings and maintains diagnostic performance within a competitive range. These results highlight FairEnc's ability to generalize fairness under distribution shifts, supporting its potential for more equitable deployment in real-world clinical settings. Our codebase and synthetic clinical notes are available at https://github.com/Mohamed-Elhabebe/FairEnc
2605.04882.paper_summary_zh: 自動化青光眼檢測對於防止不可逆的視力喪失和減輕醫療系統的負擔至關重要。<lb/>然而，確保在不同患者群體之間的公平性仍然是一個重大挑戰。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了 FairEnc，一種針對視覺-語言模型 (VLMs) 的公平預訓練方法，能夠在多個敏感屬性之間同時進行去偏見處理。<lb/>FairEnc 共同減輕了文本和視覺模態在多個敏感屬性（包括種族、性別、民族和語言）方面的偏見。<lb/>具體而言，對於文本編碼器，我們利用大型語言模型生成具有不同敏感屬性的合成臨床描述，同時保持疾病語義，並採用對比對齊目標以促進人口統計不變的表徵。<lb/>對於視覺編碼器，我們提出了一種雙層公平策略，結合了互信息正則化以減少學習特徵與人口統計群體之間的統計依賴，並進行多鑑別器對抗去偏見處理。<lb/>在公開可用的 Harvard-FairVLMed 數據集上進行的綜合實驗表明，FairEnc 能有效減少以 DPD 和 DEOdds 測量的人口統計差異，同時在零樣本和線性探測評估下達到強大的診斷性能。<lb/>在私有的 FairFundus 數據集上進行的額外實驗顯示，FairEnc 在跨領域和跨模態設置下始終保持公平優勢，並在競爭範圍內維持診斷性能。<lb/>這些結果突顯了 FairEnc 在分佈變化下通用公平性的能力，支持其在現實臨床環境中更公平部署的潛力。<lb/>我們的代碼庫和合成臨床筆記可在 https://github.com/Mohamed-Elhabebe/FairEnc 獲得。
2605.04882.author: Mohamed Elhabebe et.al.
2605.04882.authors: Mohamed Elhabebe, Ayman El-Baz, Qing Liu
